details.user_visible_error: 이 스레드는 존재하지 않습니다 (This thread does not exist)

network_observations.cors_error: Access-Control-Allow-Origin header missing on redirected script

console_errors: CORS policy: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header on requested resource

analysis: APF intercepted and blocked the Perplexity request. The thread was created with a 'blocked-' prefix ID (blocked-072e27c2-...), but when Perplexity tried to fetch the thread content, it received 400 Bad Request. The SSE 6-event template response was NOT parsed by Perplexity's frontend — instead the client treated the blocked thread as non-existent, showed a toast '이 스레드는 존재하지 않습니다', and redirected to the home page. The warning text from the SSE template was never rendered. The upgrade from 422 JSON to SSE 6-event did not improve warning visibility.
recommendation: The SSE 6-event template content is not reaching Perplexity's rendering engine. The thread 'blocked-' ID pattern causes Perplexity to treat it as an invalid thread. Consider: (1) Return a valid thread ID structure so Perplexity's client can parse the thread content, (2) Inject warning via a different mechanism — possibly modifying the initial search submission response rather than the thread fetch, (3) Use DOM injection approach post-block.
